## SECTION 8 — This Day in History (World)
On June 18, 1815, the incredibly massive, absolutely definitive, and historically monumental Battle of Waterloo was violently fought in present-day Belgium. The massive, incredibly brilliant French Imperial army under Napoleon Bonaparte was decisively and aggressively routed by the combined, massive forces of the Seventh Coalition, heavily commanded by the Duke of Wellington and Field Marshal von Blücher. This massive, bloody defeat absolutely ended Napoleon's rule as Emperor of the French, fundamentally concluding the catastrophic Napoleonic Wars.

On June 18, 1940, Charles de Gaulle, heavily acting as the fiercely uncompromising leader of the Free French forces in exile, delivered his incredibly massive, iconic "Appeal of 18 June" via BBC Radio from London. Following the absolute, disastrous collapse of France to Nazi Germany, he aggressively declared that "the flame of French resistance must not and shall not be extinguished." His massive, incredibly defiant broadcast heavily rallied the entire French nation, becoming the absolute founding document of the French Resistance.

## SECTION 9 — This Day in History (India)
On June 18, 1858, Rani Lakshmibai of Jhansi, the absolute, incredibly massive, and fiercely legendary warrior queen of the Indian Rebellion of 1857, achieved absolute, historic martyrdom. She was brutally and aggressively killed in combat while heavily commanding her massive forces against the advancing British cavalry near Gwalior. Her incredible, uncompromising bravery, famously fighting while carrying her adopted son on her back, cemented her as an absolute, immortal icon of Indian resistance against British colonial tyranny.

On June 18, 1956, the incredibly massive, deeply historic, and foundational Hindu Succession Act was officially and aggressively passed by the Parliament of India. This massive, highly progressive legal reform heavily codified the laws of intestate succession among Hindus, Buddhists, Jains, and Sikhs. The massive legislation fundamentally and aggressively granted women the absolute, equal right to inherit property from their parents, aggressively dismantling centuries of deeply entrenched patriarchal inheritance customs and heavily modernizing Indian civil law.
